type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
In previous years, we had been including emissions from mobile equipment combustion of diesel and gasoline. We only recently realized that emissions from mobile equipment are not supposed to be reported to NPRI. 

September 12, 2023 Comments/Updates:
1.Minor reduction in NO2 point/stack emissions due to use of more accurate (model specific) emissions factor for the incinerator we have at Brucejack Mine.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
September 12, 2023 Comments/Updates:
1. Update HCB emissions based on a newly found emissions factor specific to the model of incinerator that we have a Brucejack Mine. Previous emissions factor (from US EPA WebFIRE database) seems to have significantly overestimated emissions from our incinerator.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: In previous years, we had been including emissions from mobile equipment combustion of diesel and gasoline. We only recently realized that emissions from mobile equipment are not supposed to be reported to NPRI.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
September 12, 2023 Comments/Updates:
1. Update Dioxin and Furan emissions based on a newly found emissions factor specific to the model of incinerator that we have a Brucejack Mine. Previous emissions factor (from US EPA WebFIRE database) seems to have significantly overestimated emissions from our incinerator.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Road dust suppression technique change from watering to chemical suppression; however, the chemical suppressant was only applied approximately halfway through the snow-free period. Estimation of rock crushing emissions changed due to less detailed information being available for 2021. 

September 12, 2023 Comments/Updates:
1. Minor reduction in TPM point/stack emissions due to use of more accurate (model specific) emissions factor for the incinerator we have at Brucejack Mine. 

2. Reduction in road dust emissions due to calculation correction. There were more days where the ground was snow covered and/or wet or days with precipitation >0.2mm in 2021 compared to 2020. This resulted in less road dust in 2021. Dust suppression efficiency was also greater in 2021 due to use of chemical suppressant for part of the summer. 

3. Redistributed the estimated breakdown of annual releases to be primarily during summer months, as most TPM emissions were the result of road dust (which only occurred during the months of July to September) and quarry rock crushing (which only occurred during the months of August to October).

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Road dust suppression technique change from watering to chemical suppression; however, the chemical suppressant was only applied approximately halfway through the snow-free period. Estimation of rock crushing emissions changed due to less detailed information being available for 2021. 

September 12, 2023 Comments/Updates:
1. Minor reduction in PM 10 point/stack emissions due to use of more accurate (model specific) emissions factor for the incinerator we have at Brucejack Mine.
 
2. Reduction in road dust emissions due to calculation correction. There were more days where the ground was snow covered and/or wet or days with precipitation >0.2mm in 2021 compared to 2020. This resulted in less road dust in 2021. Dust suppression efficiency was also greater in 2021 due to use of chemical suppressant for part of the summer. 

3. Redistributed the estimated breakdown of annual releases to be primarily during summer months, as most PM 10 emissions were the result of road dust (which only occurred during the months of July to September) and quarry rock crushing (which only occurred during the months of August to October).

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Road dust suppression technique change from watering to chemical suppression; however, the chemical suppressant was only applied approximately halfway through the snow-free period. Estimation of rock crushing emissions changed due to less detailed information being available for 2021. 

September 12, 2023 Comments/Updates:
1. Reduction in road dust emissions due to calculation correction. There were more days where the ground was snow covered and/or wet or days with precipitation >0.2mm in 2021 compared to 2020. This resulted in less road dust in 2021. Dust suppression efficiency was also greater in 2021 due to use of chemical suppressant for part of the summer. 

2. Redistributed the estimated breakdown of annual releases to be primarily during summer months, as most PM 2.5 emissions were the result of road dust (which only occurred during the months of July to September) and quarry rock crushing (which only occurred during the months of August to October).

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Releases assumed to be evenly distributed throughout the year.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Decrease in hexachlorobenzene released in 2023 relative to 2022 due to less waste being incinerated in 2023. 

Breakdown of 2023 releases estimated based on percent of total annual mass of waste incinerated per quarter.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Releases assumed to be evenly distributed throughout the year.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Decrease in total dioxins and furans released in 2023 relative to 2022 due to less waste being incinerated in 2023. 

Breakdown of 2023 releases estimated based on percent of total annual mass of waste incinerated per quarter.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The above distribution of releases is a rough estimate only. The vast majority of TPM releases came from road dust, which primarily occurred in July and August, and to a lesser extent in the fall. 

Reduction in TPM emissions in 2023 relative to 2022 was primarily due to the application of a chemical suppressant (calcium chloride) to the road surface in early-mid June 2023, whereas there was no chemical suppressant applied in 2022.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The above distribution of releases is a rough estimate only. The vast majority of PM10 releases came from road dust, which primarily occurred in July and August, and to a lesser extent in the fall. 

Reduction in PM10 emissions in 2023 relative to 2022 was primarily due to the application of a chemical suppressant (calcium chloride) to the road surface in early-mid June 2023, whereas there was no chemical suppressant applied in 2022.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The above distribution of releases is a rough estimate only. The majority of PM2.5 releases came from road dust, which primarily occurred in July and August, and to a lesser extent in the fall. 

Reduction in PM2.5 emissions in 2023 relative to 2022 was primarily due to the application of a chemical suppressant (calcium chloride) to the road surface in early-mid June 2023, whereas there was no chemical suppressant applied in 2022.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Lower NOx emissions in 2024 relative to 2023 resulted from less emulsion being used on site (because there was less waste rock and ore blasted in 2024) as well as less diesel generator use due to fewer hydroelectric powerline outages in 2024 relative to 2023.

The monthly breakdown of annual emissions is a rough estimate, based on the assumption of emissions being evenly distributed throughout the year.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The increase in HCB emissions in 2024 relative to 2023 was due to a significantly larger quantity of municipal waste being incinerated in 2024 compared to 2023. 

Breakdown of 2024 releases estimated based on percent of total annual mass of waste incinerated per quarter.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Lower NOx emissions in 2024 relative to 2023 resulted from less emulsion being used on site (because there was less waste rock and ore blasted in 2024) as well as less diesel generator use due to fewer hydroelectric powerline outages in 2024 relative to 2023.

The monthly breakdown of annual emissions is a rough estimate, based on the assumption of emissions being evenly distributed throughout the year.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The increase in Dioxins and Furans emissions in 2024 relative to 2023 was due to a significantly larger quantity of municipal waste being incinerated in 2024 compared to 2023. 

Breakdown of 2024 releases estimated based on percent of total annual mass of waste incinerated per quarter.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The vast majority of TPM emissions were attributable to road dust. A lower dust control efficiency was used in 2024 calculations than 2023 calculations (7% in 2024 versus 60% in 2023) due to no chemical suppressant being applied to the roads at Brucejack Camp in 2024. 

Monthly distribution of emissions is a rough estimate only. The vast majority of annual TPM emissions were attributable to road dust, which was generated primarily in summer months (July/August/September).

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The vast majority of PM10 emissions were attributable to road dust. A lower dust control efficiency was used in 2024 calculations than 2023 calculations (7% in 2024 versus 60% in 2023) due to no chemical suppressant being applied to the roads at Brucejack Camp in 2024. 

Monthly distribution of emissions is a rough estimate only. The vast majority of annual PM10 emissions were attributable to road dust, which was generated primarily in summer months (July/August/September).

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Monthly distribution of emissions is a rough estimate only. About 88% of annual PM2.5 emissions were attributable to road dust, which was generated primarily in summer months (July/August/September).

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: We ran back-up diesel generators for more hours in 2022 than we did in 2021.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Last year, a published emission factor from the US EPA WebFIRE database was used. Based on further research, it seems likely that the emission factor from the US EPA WebFIRE database resulted in overestimating the total HCB emitted by our incinerator. This year, we found and used an emission factor specific to the model of incinerator that we have on site. The emission factor was published by Environment Canada in a 2003 emissions test report.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: We ran back-up diesel generators for more hours in 2022 than we did in 2021.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Last year, a published emission factor from the US EPA WebFIRE database was used. Based on further research, it seems likely that the emission factor from the US EPA WebFIRE database resulted in overestimating the total dioxins and furans emitted by our incinerator. This year, we found and used an emission factor specific to the model of incinerator that we have on site. The emission factor was published by Environment Canada in a 2003 emissions test report.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Calculation error in 2021 resulted in underestimation of days with snow cover or >0.2mm of precipitation, which resulted in a significant overestimation of road dust emissions. 

Breakdown of annual releases is an estimate only. Most TPM emissions are a result of road dust, which only occurs during summer months.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Calculation error in 2021 resulted in underestimation of days with snow cover or >0.2mm of precipitation, which resulted in a significant overestimation of road dust emissions. 

Breakdown of annual releases is an estimate only. Most PM 10 emissions are a result of road dust, which only occurs during summer months.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Calculation error in 2021 resulted in underestimation of days with snow cover or >0.2mm of precipitation, which resulted in a significant overestimation of road dust emissions. 

Breakdown of annual releases is an estimate only. Most PM 2.5 emissions are a result of road dust, which only occurs during summer months.
